question: Looking at the US unemployment rate map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: The unemployment rate differences are impossible to determine from this visualization.
options.B: The unemployment rate gap between South Carolina and Arizona might exceed that between New Mexico and Missouri.
options.C: South Carolina and Arizona have very different unemployment rate levels.
options.D: South Carolina and Arizona have similar unemployment rates.

explanation: South Carolina (6.1%) and Arizona (7.8%) have a 1.7% unemployment rate difference, while New Mexico (1.8%) and Missouri (2.4%) have only a 0.6% difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups South Carolina and Arizona into the same color bin (6-8% range), making their large difference appear similar, while New Mexico and Missouri appear more different due to different color bins.
